## BottomlessWater 3.4.2

API-compatibility fix for the current Rust + Oxide build (verified against Oxide 2.0.7423).

### Fixed
- **Admin console commands restored.** Rust changed `ConsoleSystem.Arg.Args` from
  `string[]` to `Facepunch.StringView[]`, which prevented the plugin from compiling —
  and therefore from loading — on the affected build. `bottomlesswater.toggle` and
  `bottomlesswater.status` now read their arguments via `arg.GetString(i)`, which
  returns a `string` and is stable across the change. Runtime behaviour is unchanged.

### Build / tooling
- New out-of-server compile-validation chain — `build/BottomlessWater.csproj`,
  `tools/fetch-references.sh` / `.ps1`, `.github/workflows/compile.yml`, `Makefile`,
  and `BUILD.md` — that type-checks the plugin against the real Oxide/Rust/Unity
  assemblies on every push and PR. This is the chain that caught the break above.

### Install / update
Drop `BottomlessWater.cs` into your server's `oxide/plugins/` directory and run
`oxide.reload BottomlessWater`. No config changes are required. See `INSTALL.md`.
